data.title: Victoria’s Secret beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; gives Q4 and raises FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Victoria’s Secret press release (VSCO): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.27 beats by $0.32.
  • Revenue of $1.47B (+8.9% Y/Y) beats by $60M.
  • Total comparable sales for the third quarter of 2025 increased 8%.

  • Full Year and Fourth Quarter 2025 OutlookThe Company is raising its full year outlook and is now forecasting net sales in the range of $6.450 billion to $6.480 billion (vs. consensus of $6.39B), compared to prior guidance of $6.330 billion to $6.410 billion. At this forecasted level of net sales, adjusted operating income is expected to be in the range of $350 million to $375 million, compared to prior guidance of $270 million to $320 million. Adjusted net income per diluted share for the full year 2025 is estimated to be in the range of $2.40 to $2.65 (vs. consensus of $2.13), compared to prior guidance of $1.80 to $2.20. This outlook includes an estimated net tariff impact of approximately $90 million for fiscal year 2025, compared to prior guidance of $100 million.

  • For the fourth quarter, the Company is forecasting net sales to be in the range of $2.170 billion to $2.200 billion (vs. consensus of $2.17) compared to last year’s fourth quarter net sales of $2.106 billion. At this forecasted level of net sales, adjusted operating income for the fourth quarter of 2025 is expected to be in the range of $265 million to $290 million. Adjusted net income per diluted share for the fourth quarter of 2025 is estimated to be in the range of $2.20 to $2.45 . As previously disclosed, last year’s results include an approximately $26 million benefit to net sales, gross margin and operating income as a result of the Company’s cumulative adjustment for its change in accounting estimate related to expected future redemption on outstanding gift cards.

data.title: Netflix to buy Warner Bros. Discovery in $82.7B deal
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) said it will acquire Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), including its film and television studios, HBO Max and HBO, for a total enterprise value of ~$82.7 billion.

The streaming gain will buy WBD for $27.75 per share.

data.title: Apple iPhone assembler Foxconn posts 25.53% November revenue gain despite sequential decline
data.description:
Apple's contract manufacturers Foxconn Technology (OTCPK:FXCOF) — which is formerly known as Hon Hai Precision (OTCPK:HNHAF) (OTCPK:HNHPF) has reported revenue of NT$844.28 billion for November 2025, representing a decrease of 5.74% M/M and an increase of 25.53% Y/Y.

The Taiwan-based company said cumulative revenue for the first eleven months of 2025 jumped 16.63% year-over-year to NT$7.24T.

See below detailing Foxconn's monthly revenue performance in 2025:

data.title: Trimble sets $1B stock repurchase plan
data.description:
• Trimble (TRMB) board of directors authorized the repurchase of up to $1.0 billion in shares of the company's common stock.
  • The program does not have an expiration date and replaces the prior authorization of up to $1.0 billion, of which $273 million was remaining as of the end of the third quarter of 2025, but is now cancelled.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ITT to acquire SPX FLOW for $4.775B in cash & equity
data.description:
ITT (ITT) has entered into a definitive agreement with Lone Star Funds to acquire SPX FLOW for a total consideration of $4.775 billion in cash and equity.

This represents 14.2x SPX FLOW’s estimated full-year 2026 adjusted EBITDA, or 11.5x including expected cost synergies. The consideration will consist of a combination of cash and $700 million in ITT common stock issued to Lone Star.

ITT intends to fund the cash portion of the transaction through a combination of debt and equity.

SPX FLOW provides highly engineered equipment and process technologies for industrial, health, and nutrition sectors. “The acquisition of SPX FLOW adds critical equipment and adjacent flow and process technologies that extend ITT’s capabilities to address complex customer challenges across growing end markets with secular trends, including industrial, chemical, energy, mining, nutrition and health and personal care,” ITT (ITT) said.

The transaction is expected to close by the end of the first quarter of 2026 and is expected to be immediately accretive to ITT’s gross margin and adjusted EBITDA margin, with adjusted EPS accretion anticipated in 2026 and double digits anticipated during the first full year post-close, excluding amortization of intangibles.

ITT -2.49% premarket.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Southwest Airlines falls after warning on EBIT hit from government shutdown
data.description:
Southwest Airlines Co. (LUV) traded lower after the company provided a guidance update on Friday. 

The airline operator said that as a result of lower revenue due to the government shutdown and the impact of higher fuel prices, it now expects its full-year 2025 EBIT to be approximately $500 million, compared with its prior expectation for a range of $600 million to $800 million. No other guidance information was released in the SEC filing.

Southwest Airlines (LUV) highlighted that following the temporary decline in demand related to the shutdown, bookings have now returned to previous expectations.

Shares of Southwest Airlines (LUV) were down 1.7% in the premarket session to $35.20 vs. the 52-week trading range of $23.83 to $37.96. The Seeking Alpha Quant Rating on LUV is flashing Buy and its has the 4th best quant score in the airline sector.

data.title: Laurentian Bank of Canada Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.73, revenue of C$244.71M
data.description:
• Laurentian Bank of Canada press release (LRCDF): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.73.
  • Revenue of C$244.71M (-2.4% Y/Y).

data.title: Capricor Therapeutics prices $150M stock offering
data.description:
• Capricor Therapeutics (CAPR) shares popped over 4% premarket on Friday after the biotechnology company priced an offering to raise $150M.
  • The offering includes 6M shares of common stock issued at a public offering price of $25.00 per share. Capricor also granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 900K shares.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for the continued development and manufacturing of its product candidates, for working capital, and for general corporate purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 8, 2025, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.
  • CAPR +4.8% premarket to $26.63
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Airbus faces steep December push as technical snags threaten delivery goal
data.description:
Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) faces a steep climb to close out the year, needing to hand over 133 aircraft in December to meet its updated annual delivery goal. The challenge comes as the manufacturer deals with an unusual cluster of technical issues that could pull attention away from production.

The company reported Friday that it delivered 72 jets in November, bringing the year-to-date total to 657. Hitting the target is still possible, as Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) managed 123 deliveries last December alone, but this year’s final stretch is being complicated by quality concerns tied to the A320 family.

The past several days have underscored the pressure. On Wednesday, Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) cut its delivery guidance by 30 aircraft. Days earlier, airlines worldwide were forced to rush through a software patch affecting more than 6,000 A320-series jets after engineers discovered that solar radiation could interfere with flight-control systems. And on Monday, the company disclosed that it must examine several hundred A320s for potentially faulty fuselage panels.

Despite the turbulence, Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) continued to build its order book. In November it logged 75 new gross orders, including a fresh commitment from IndiGo for 30 A350-900 widebodies, lifting the Indian carrier’s Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) backlog to 919 aircraft.

data.title: Putin: If U.S. has the right to buy fuel from us, why shouldn't India?
data.description:
Russia's President Vladimir Putin asserted that India should enjoy the same privilege as the U.S. for buying Russian fuel, according to an interview with India Today that aired hours after he landed in New Delhi.

"The U.S. continues to buy nuclear fuel from us for their nuclear power plants," Putin pointed out. "That is also fuel. Energy. This is uranium for nuclear power plants that are functioning in the U.S."

"If the U.S. has the right to purchase fuel from us, why should India be deprived of such a right? That is a matter to be studied closely, and we are prepared to discuss that and debate that with President Trump," he added.

Russia currently supplies around 20%-30% of enriched uranium product used in the U.S. and Europe, according to the Department of Energy. The U.S. will completely ban all uranium imports from Russia by 2028.

The Trump administration imposed punitive tariffs on Indian goods because of India's ongoing purchases of Russian oil. Since then, India has scaled back these imports.

The Russian leader arrived in India on Thursday for a two-day state visit, his first trip to the country in four years. 

Putin and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi agreed to expand economic cooperation across sectors from defense to shipping, and accelerate progress toward the $100B bilateral trade target by 2030.

Putin also said Russia was "ready to continue uninterrupted fuel shipments" to India.
